No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course directly teaches psychophysiological assessment and biofeedback therapy for 'clinical symptoms or disorders,' strongly aligning with SDG 3.4 on promoting mental health and well-being.
evidence: biofeedback treatment procedures (生理回饋治療程序上)
clinical symptoms or disorders (臨床症狀或疾患的處理上)

Quality Education.reason: As a graduate-level university course (Psychology/M/1), it directly contributes to quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops professional competence for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus addresses gender equality.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops 'Professional competence in psychology,' which prepares students for future productive employment and decent work, aligning with Target 8.5.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course involves 'Introduction to the Biofeedback Equipment' and 'instrument practice,' directly enhancing scientific and technological capabilities, aligning with Target 9.5.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: While 'Multiculturalism' is a core competency, the course content does not explicitly address reducing broader inequalities (e.g., income, age, disability).
